narrative.headline: 2020 Toyota Prius: brakes lead a small set of owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 14 complaints with NHTSA. Service brakes are the most reported area at 29%, followed by the electrical system and tires (14% each). No fires or deaths are reported, but every brake complaint involved a crash, so the brakes are worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.sample: MY DAD WAS DRIVING ON THE HIGHWAY. HE SAID THAT HE STEPPED ON THE BRAKES AND THE BRAKES DID NOT WORK. HE GOT INTO AN ACCIDENT IN WHICH THE ALL AIRBAGS WERE DEPLOYED AND THE CAR WAS TOTALED.  THE CAR ONLY HAD 700 MILES.  I BELIEVE THIS WAS THE SECOND TIME THAT MY DAD DROVE THIS CA
issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: The top complaint: 4 reports (29%), and all 4 involved a crash, with 3 injuries. Test the brakes carefully before buying.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 2 reports (14%), one of which involved a crash and 1 injury. Watch for unexpected electrical behavior.

issues.blurb: 2 reports (14%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries noted. A minor complaint area.
